web.title: List of countries by uranium reserves - Wikipedia
web.description: Australia is estimated to have the largest reserves, followed by Kazakhstan, Canada and Russia.
web.markdown:
[Jump to content](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_uranium_reserves#bodyContent) From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Uranium_Reserves.png/500px-Uranium_Reserves.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Uranium_Reserves.png) World uranium reserves in 2010 **Uranium reserves** are reserves of recoverable [uranium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uranium "Uranium"), regardless of [isotope](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isotope "Isotope"), based on a set market price. The list given here is based on _Uranium 2020: Resources, Production and Demand_, a joint report by the [OECD Nuclear Energy Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OECD_Nuclear_Energy_Agency "OECD Nuclear Energy Agency") and the [International Atomic Energy Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Atomic_Energy_Agency "International Atomic Energy Agency").[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_uranium_reserves#cite_note-ur_2020-1) Figures are given in metric [tonnes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tonne "Tonne"). The reserves figures denote identified resources as of 1 January 2015, consisting of reasonably assured resources (RAR) plus inferred resources recoverable at a cost range of below US$260/kg U. The list also includes cumulative historical production figures. The amount of ultimately recoverable uranium depends strongly on what one would be willing to pay for it. Uranium is a widely distributed metal with large low-grade deposits that are not currently considered profitable. As of 2015, 646,900 tonnes of reserves are recoverable at US$40 per kilogram of uranium, while 7,641,600 tonnes of reserves are recoverable at $260 per kilogram.[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_uranium_reserves#cite_note-ur_2016-2) Moreover, much of Canada, Greenland, Siberia and Antarctica are currently unexplored due to [permafrost](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Permafrost "Permafrost") and may hold substantial undiscovered reserves. Australia is estimated to have the largest reserves, followed by Kazakhstan, Canada and Russia. However, it is worth noting that Australia does not utilise its reserves, though it mines at Olympic dam. ## List \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=List_of_countries_by_uranium_reserves&action=edit§ion=1 "Edit section: List")\] | Country | Continent | Reserves as of

Total | | 8,070,400 | 2,818,415 | **Notes:** Historical production for the Czech Republic includes 102,241 tonnes of uranium produced in former [Czechoslovakia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Czechoslovakia "Czechoslovakia") from 1946 through the end of 1992. Historical production for Germany includes 213,380 tonnes produced in the [German Democratic Republic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/German_Democratic_Republic "German Democratic Republic") from 1946 through the end of 1992. Historical production for the Soviet Union includes the former Soviet Socialist Republics of [Estonia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Estonian_Soviet_Socialist_Republic "Estonian Soviet Socialist Republic"), [Kyrgyzstan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kirghiz_Soviet_Socialist_Republic "Kirghiz Soviet Socialist Republic"), [Tajikistan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tajik_SSR "Tajik SSR") and [Uzbekistan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uzbek_Soviet_Socialist_Republic "Uzbek Soviet Socialist Republic"), but excludes Kazakhstan and Ukraine. Historical production for the Russian Federation and Uzbekistan is since 1992 only. ## See also \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=List_of_countries_by_uranium_reserves&action=edit§ion=2 "Edit section: See also")\] - [Uranium mining](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uranium_mining "Uranium mining") - [List of countries by uranium production](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_uranium_production "List of countries by uranium production") - [Uranium mining by country](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uranium_mining_by_country "Uranium mining by country") ## References \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=List_of_countries_by_uranium_reserves&action=edit§ion=3 "Edit section: References")\] 1. **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_uranium_reserves#cite_ref-ur_2020_1-0 "Jump up")**[_Uranium 2020: Resources, Production and Demand_](https://www.oecd-nea.org/upload/docs/application/pdf/2020-12/7555_uranium_-_resources_production_and_demand_2020__web.pdf)(PDF).
